---
title: "EasternCanadaDataPrep Manual"
subtitle: "v.`r SpaDES.core::moduleMetadata(module = 'EasternCanadaDataPrep', path = '..')$version`"
date: "Last updated: `r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  bookdown::html_document2:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: sandstone
    number_sections: false
    df_print: paged
    keep_md: yes
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
bibliography: citations/references_EasternCanadaDataPrep.bib
link-citations: true
always_allow_html: true
---

# EasternCanadaDataPrep Module

<!-- the following are text references used in captions for LaTeX compatibility -->
(ref:EasternCanadaDataPrep) *EasternCanadaDataPrep*

```{r setup-EasternCanadaDataPrep, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, eval = FALSE, results = "hold") ## change to eval = TRUE if all chunks are to be evaluated

dir.create("figures", showWarnings = FALSE)

download.file(url = "https://img.shields.io/badge/Made%20with-Markdown-1f425f.png",
              destfile = "figures/markdownBadge.png",
              mode = "wb")
```

[![made-with-Markdown](figures/markdownBadge.png)](https://commonmark.org)

<!-- if knitting to pdf remember to add the pandoc_args: ["--extract-media", "."] option to yml in order to get the badge images -->

#### Authors:

`r paste(as.character(SpaDES.core::moduleMetadata(module = "EasternCanadaDataPrep", path = '..')$authors), sep = ', ')`
<!-- ideally separate authors with new lines, '\n' not working -->

## Module Overview

### Module summary

This module prepares all spatial data layers required for the Eastern Canada
landbase. It automatically downloads (when sourceURLs are provided), extracts,
crops, masks, reprojects, and standardizes multiple raw datasets into a
consistent geospatial framework.

The module processes layers such as Protected Areas (CPCAD), Land Cover (LCC
2020), Forest Management Units (FMUs), hydrology (NHN/CHN), and the study area
boundary. All outputs are harmonized in a common projection (NAD83 / Conus
Albers – EPSG:5070) and saved in formats ready for downstream use in LandR,
AAC calculation, harvest modules, and classification routines.


### Module inputs and parameters

This module requires several raw spatial datasets, which may either be provided
locally or downloaded via `prepInputs()` when a sourceURL is specified. These
include the study area polygon, CPCAD protected areas, land cover rasters,
FMU boundaries, and hydrology layers.

The following tables are automatically generated from the module metadata and
list all input objects and user-visible parameters.


```{r moduleInputs-EasternCanadaDataPrep, echo = FALSE, eval = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
df_inputs <- SpaDES.core::moduleInputs("EasternCanadaDataPrep", "E:/EasternCanadaProject")
knitr::kable(df_inputs,
             caption = "List of (ref:EasternCanadaDataPrep) input objects and their description.") |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = "scale_down", full_width = TRUE)
```

Summary of user-visible parameters (Table \@ref(tab:moduleParams-EasternCanadaDataPrep))


```{r moduleParams-EasternCanadaDataPrep, echo = FALSE, eval = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
df_params <- SpaDES.core::moduleParams("EasternCanadaDataPrep", "E:/EasternCanadaProject")
knitr::kable(df_params, caption = "List of (ref:EasternCanadaDataPrep) parameters and their description.") |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = "scale_down", full_width = TRUE)
```

### Events

The module uses the standard SpaDES event structure. Each event is responsible
for a well-defined stage of the data preparation workflow:

#### init
Initializes the module by setting up paths, loading or generating the study area
object, and preparing any module-level variables required for downstream events.

#### download
Downloads all raw spatial datasets that define a `sourceURL` or loads them from
the local cache if they already exist. This includes CPCAD, LCC2020, FMUs, and
NHN/CHN hydrology layers. Archives are extracted and validated.

#### prep
Processes all raw datasets by cropping and masking them to the study area,
reprojecting them to the target CRS (EPSG:5070), cleaning attributes, and
standardizing formats. This event produces the harmonized spatial layers used
by subsequent modules.

#### save
Writes the cleaned and standardized spatial layers to the module’s designated
outputPath, ensuring that each dataset is reproducible and consistently named.

### Plotting

This module does not generate plots by default. Optional diagnostic visualizations
(such as quick maps of the study area or processed layers) may be added for
quality assurance if needed.

### Saving

The module saves all processed spatial layers in standardized geospatial formats,
suitable for downstream modeling. Typical outputs include:

• Processed CPCAD protected areas (e.g., `cpcad_5070.gpkg`)
• Processed land cover raster (e.g., `lcc2020_5070.tif`)
• Forest Management Unit boundaries (vector or raster)
• Hydrology data (e.g., `flowlines_5070.gpkg`)
• Study area polygon (`studyArea_5070.gpkg`)

All files are written to the simulation `outputPath` defined by the user.

### Module outputs

Description of the module outputs (Table \@ref(tab:moduleOutputs-EasternCanadaDataPrep)).

```{r moduleOutputs-EasternCanadaDataPrep, echo = FALSE, eval = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
df_outputs <- SpaDES.core::moduleOutputs("EasternCanadaDataPrep", "E:/EasternCanadaProject")
knitr::kable(df_outputs, caption = "List of (ref:EasternCanadaDataPrep) outputs and their description.") |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = "scale_down", full_width = TRUE)
```

### Links to other modules

This module provides foundational spatial layers required by multiple downstream
modules, including:

• LandR Biomass (pixel-group initialization)
• AAC (Allowable Annual Cut) calculation module
• SimpleHarvest / block-based harvest modules
• Forest classification modules for yield curve assignment

Because these modules depend on a harmonized spatial landbase, the
EasternCanadaDataPrep module must be run before starting any forest dynamics,
classification, or harvest simulations.

### Getting help

For questions, bug reports, or feature requests:

• GitHub issues:
  https://github.com/shirinvark/EasternCanadaDataPrep/issues

• SpaDES user support (Zulip):
  https://spades.zulipchat.com

• SpaDES documentation:
  https://spades-core.predictiveecology.org

## References

<!-- autogenerated from bibligraphy -->
